mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending forecasts and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in consumer and capital demand, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression models over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion phases reflects maximum capacity utilization and strong corporate pricing power, generating short-term positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns during early-cycle or recession regimes, or fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls growth exceeding 150,000 jobs in a single month confirms robust economic activity and wage growth, boosting near-term corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of threshold-crossing months during out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth rates reduce aggregate corporate profit forecasts and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns occur in more than 50% of periods with negative GDP growth momentum during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while unemployment rises indicates simultaneous supply-side contraction and labor slack, jointly predicting negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in walk-forward testing or produces positive returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, initial federal funds rate increases are interpreted as growth-supportive policy normalization, generating short-term positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative in the first 5 trading days post-hike or Rank IC falls below 0.02 in tightening regimes.
